data.body: SUBSUMES 39c5 (grungy-grain-drawl), linked 39c5 duplicates e3d0 — 39c5 closed as a duplicate. e3d0 is the broader superset (operationPack-style batched write adopted on the highest-churn paths: import + reconciler apply + multi-event txns). Folding in 39c5's two distinctive details so nothing is lost: (1) the concrete primitive shape `_store.batch_stage_and_commit(events)` — ONE write lock, M event files each with its own unique name (so union-merge + UUID-keyed SNAPSHOT compaction are unaffected), one git add, ONE commit; single-event write path unchanged. (2) Crash-resume requirement: a partial staging before commit must be recoverable because import is idempotent (resume by source_id) — add atomicity + crash-resume tests alongside the union-merge/compaction regression tests. 39c5 also noted the importer-only slice can be a sensible first increment of e3d0.
